title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men are charged with kidnapping and multiple assaults after allegedly attacking a man near Winter Park.
The suspects allegedly beat the victim, tied him with an extension cord, and recorded the incident on video.
The men have been released on bail and face arraignment on charges including kidnapping and assault with a dangerous weapon.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Two individuals were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport after attending events in Boston.
Maria Rosales, a DACA recipient, and David Ardila, an asylum seeker, were detained on allegedly overstaying visa issues.
Attorney Todd Pomerleau filed legal challenges for their release, citing lengthy processing times for immigration benefits.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Interstellar" (2014) parallels "The Odyssey" with a NASA pilot's cosmic journey to reunite with family. (Hulu, Paramount+)
"Lawrence of Arabia" (1962) and "O Brother, Where Art Thou?" (2000) are inspired by themes from "The Odyssey." (Netflix, Criterion Channel)
"The Searchers" (1956) and "The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie" (2004) reflect elements of "The Odyssey" in their narratives. (Criterion Channel, Paramount+)

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of the Rays.
Boston now holds the third AL wild-card spot, improving to .500 for the first time since March.
Jarren Duran and Wilyer Abreu lead strong offensive performances, supported by a solid bullpen effort.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are progressing well in their recoveries, with Suarez possibly returning July 21.
Chad Tracy reports Kutter Crawford and Tanner Houck are making strides; Houck is throwing twice weekly post-Tommy John surgery.
Johan Oviedo nears a comeback, preparing for a bullpen session after his elbow strain, while other injured players have no updates.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and an RBI, leading Boston to success in Game 1 of Friday's doubleheader.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 across two games, putting consistent pressure on Tampa Bay's pitchers without flashy plays.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings, allowing one hit and walk, crucially conserving the bullpen for Game 2.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Patriots coach Mike Vrabel after offseason controversy involving NFL reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel is the Patriots' third head coach since 2023, following Bill Belichick and Jerod Mayo's departures.
Patriots set to begin training camp next Saturday in Foxborough after last year's Super Bowl loss to Seattle.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Sam Neill stars in 'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople' now streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
'Obsession', the 2026 horror hit, is available for streaming on Peacock.
'Lucky' Season 1 featuring Anya Taylor-Joy is now on Apple TV.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, from Concord, assaulted a transgender woman in a bias-motivated attack in May 2024.
The Merrimack County Superior Court found Lufkin violated New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act due to gender identity animus.
Lufkin received a $5,000 fine, with $4,000 suspended, and was restricted from contact with the victim for three years.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox chief baseball officer Craig Breslow considers options as MLB trade deadline approaches on August 3.
Red Sox aims to extend winning streak and improve playoff odds, currently at 44.3%, following recent victory.
Trade decision may involve keeping closer Aroldis Chapman, with focus on acquiring right-handed hitter as potential buyers.
